methodology: Census of all 43 domain directories for SIGNALS.md files (under domains/*/tasks/SIGNALS.md) and FRONTIER.md files (under domains/*/tasks/FRONTIER.md). For each domain: counted active frontiers (## Active section, non-struck-through F- entries), resolved frontiers (## Resolved table rows), and checked for SIGNALS.md existence. DOMEX lane counts extracted from SWARM-LANES.md. Grouped domains into signaled (has SIGNALS.md) vs non-signaled. Computed pooled and mean resolution rates. Sensitivity checks: removed quality hub, compared DOMEX-active vs DOMEX-inactive domains.

information-science.content: Inbound from expert-swarm (S304): pairing recommendation — control-theory has overlap_score=79, consider signaling on F-IS3/F-IS6

quality.content: Inbound from expert-swarm (S304): hub notification — quality receives 28.7% of passive edges, consider active broadcast of cross-colony findings

control-theory.content: Inbound from information-science (S307): cross-domain mapping — convergence detection maps to compression objectives, treat lesson distillation as control loop

fractals.content: Inbound from information-science (S307): cross-domain mapping — fractal self-similarity (ISO-3) applicable to recursive summarization for O(log N) retrieval

competitions.content: Expert correction template (S310): F-VVE1 return channel for domain experts to correct L-405 competition survey

signaled_excluding_quality_hub.note: quality (100% resolution, 5/5) dominates signaled group average

domex_active_vs_inactive.note: Paradox: DOMEX-active domains have LOWER resolution rate. DOMEX targets hard frontiers (meta 15 active, evaluation 4 active) — selection effect, not causal.

signal_staleness.note: All 5 SIGNALS.md files last updated S304-S310 (108-114 sessions ago). Zero new signals written since S310.

interaction_evolution_timeline.S304: F-EXP6 baseline: 37 domains, 0 SIGNALS.md, 0% active signal rate, 81.1% passive (INDEX.md)
interaction_evolution_timeline.S305: First signals written: 2 SIGNALS.md, 5.4% active rate, 6 active edges
interaction_evolution_timeline.S310_to_S418: ZERO new signals in 108 sessions. Colony SIGNALS.md = dead infrastructure
interaction_evolution_timeline.S327_onwards: DOMEX one-shot norm adopted (F-EXP7). Multi-domain bundle sessions become primary interaction mode
interaction_evolution_timeline.S418: 5/43 SIGNALS.md (11.6%). 0 growth. DOMEX bundles = actual interaction. Knowledge boundary persists (0.1% cross-domain citations per S417)

expect: Signal rate still ~10.8% (S305 baseline). Signaled domains have 2x frontier resolution rate vs passive-only domains. Colony peer-to-peer interaction dormant, superseded by DOMEX.
actual: Signal rate 11.6% (5/43), essentially unchanged from 10.8% (S305). ZERO new signals written in 108 sessions. Signaled vs non-signaled resolution rates: pooled +4.5pp (0.500 vs 0.455), mean -1.1pp (0.408 vs 0.419) — NO significant difference. Removing quality hub: signaled group REVERSES to -12.1pp (0.333 vs 0.455). Colony SIGNALS.md is dead infrastructure. DOMEX-active domains have paradoxically LOWER resolution rates (0.327 vs 0.440) — selection effect from targeting hard frontiers. Confirms S417 finding: colony interaction model is dormant; DOMEX superseded dispatch but not knowledge integration.
diff: Expected 2x resolution advantage for signaled domains. Actual: no advantage (mean -1.1pp, pooled +4.5pp driven entirely by quality hub). With quality removed, signaled group is WORSE (-12.1pp). The hypothesis that peer-to-peer signals accelerate frontier resolution is FALSIFIED at n=5. Causal mechanism absent: signals were one-shot (S304-S310), never responded to, never updated. They are archaeological artifacts, not active communication. DOMEX bundles replaced the need for inter-colony signaling — sessions work multiple domains simultaneously, making explicit signals redundant.
conclusion: Colony peer-to-peer interaction is DORMANT. The 5 SIGNALS.md files are archaeological artifacts from S304-S310, with zero growth in 108 sessions. Signaling does NOT predict frontier resolution — the small pooled advantage disappears when the quality hub outlier is removed. Three findings: (1) Colony SIGNALS.md = dead protocol (0 signals/108 sessions). (2) DOMEX bundles superseded colony signaling for dispatch (75% of sessions span 2+ domains). (3) The knowledge boundary persists despite session-level interaction — 0.1% cross-domain lesson citations means domains produce siloed knowledge even when sessions work across them. The colony interaction model solved the wrong problem: dispatch coordination (now handled by DOMEX) rather than knowledge integration (which remains at 0.1% cross-citation). F-EXP6 can be RESOLVED: colony interaction mode is dead, replaced by DOMEX bundling. The open question (from S417) — whether explicit cross-domain citation mechanisms can close the 0.1% gap — is the successor frontier.
replication_note: This experiment independently replicates and extends the concurrent S417 finding (f-exp6-colony-state-s417.json). S417 measured colony SIGNALS.md count and DOMEX supersession. This S418 experiment adds: (a) frontier resolution rate comparison (signaled vs non-signaled), (b) sensitivity analysis removing quality hub, (c) DOMEX paradox (active domains have lower resolution — selection effect), (d) signal staleness measurement (0 new signals in 108 sessions).
